name: napari (n-dimensional Image Viewer)

performance.notes: vispy/OpenGL rendering; handles large datasets via Dask/Zarr lazy loading; 3D rendering capable

agent_readiness.notes: N-dimensional image viewer for Python, built for life sciences. import napari. viewer = napari.Viewer(). viewer.add_image(np.random.rand(100, 100)). viewer.add_labels(label_array). viewer.add_points(coordinates). viewer.add_shapes([ellipse, rectangle]). napari.run(). Dask/Zarr for out-of-memory image data. Plugin ecosystem: napari-plugin-engine. 3D volumetric rendering. Time series display. Interactive annotation. magicgui for auto-generated parameter GUIs. Used in cell biology, neuroscience, medical imaging. ~300+ plugins.

gotchas: Qt dependency — requires display (not for headless servers)
Plugin compatibility can break between napari versions
Startup time can be slow on first launch
best_when: Life science imaging: cell segmentation, microscopy, neuroscience
Interactive annotation and labeling for ML training data
Building custom imaging analysis GUIs via plugins
summary: napari is an n-dimensional image viewer for Python, built for life sciences. viewer = napari.Viewer(); viewer.add_image(array); viewer.add_labels(masks). Handles out-of-memory data via Dask/Zarr. 3D volumetric rendering. 300+ plugins for cell segmentation, tracking, analysis. magicgui for auto-generated parameter UIs. Used in microscopy, neuroscience, medical imaging. Requires display (Qt). BSD-3 license. CZI-funded community project.
